title: Borders of France - Wikipedia
content: The French Republic [1] has terrestrial borders with 10 sovereign states, 8 bordering Metropolitan France [2] and 2 bordering the Overseas Departments.

title: Which Countries Border France?
content: France is one of the largest countries in Europe with a total combined area of 248,573 square miles and a total population of 67.15 million people. Being among the oldest countries in the world, France is the product of an alliance of duchies and principalities under a single ruler. France shares land boundaries with eight countries, which encompass Italy, Germany, Belgium, Spain, Monaco, Andorra, Switzerland, and Luxembourg. France has endeavored to maintain strong and mutually beneficial ties with all the neighboring states as a means of enhancing social cohesion and economic integration in the Eurozone. The Principality of Andorra is bordered by France to the North. The two countries have strong diplomatic ties, which have existed since 1278 when Andorra acquired its sovereign status through pareage by the Count of Foix in southern France. The two nations have established strong economic ties with France being Italy's second-largest trading partner and Italy being the second-largest trading partner of France.
